title: Pallenis hierochuntica
intro: Pallenis hierochuntica, also known as the Palestinian chamomile, is a flowering plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pallenis hierochuntica include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments, making it a popular herbal remedy in the region.
taxonomy: Pallenis hierochuntica is a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is native to the Middle East and North Africa. This plant is also known as the Palestine daisy or Palestine chicory. Pallenis hierochuntica is classified as a perennial herb that grows up to 30 centimeters in height. It produces small white or pink flowers in clusters at the top of its stem.
